author: Audacious
text:
§6 subprocess plugin dispatch + §7 wire payloads

Implement the lifecycle::Plugins seam as plugin::Subprocess: spawn each plugin '<bin> <op> <phase>' with cwd = the change worktree, env BALLS_PROTOCOL/PLUGIN_NAME/PLUGIN_DEPTH, the §7 payload on stdin, stderr captured to logs/<name>/, and stdout discarded — no return channel, plugins contribute by editing the worktree. A non-zero exit is an io::Error the engine turns into an abort. 'describe' runs '<bin> protocol' and parses {protocol, ops} (scalar or list version). The depth cap runs nested ops plugin-free (suppressed, not refused).

Add wire.rs: the §7 payload types (Binding, Command, FieldChange, OpContext, Payload) serialized to JSON. One OpContext holds the op-constant wire data; OpContext::wire stamps the per-phase shape — pre is states-only with no id, post advances the state pair and adds commit/previous_commit/metadata, rollback adds rolling_back. serde omits the absent fields.

Widen the lifecycle::Plugins seam: run/rollback now carry Option<Sealed> (the sealed commit, previous tip, and §5 message) so post reactors and post-phase rollbacks get the §7 post facts. The engine threads a SealRecord through its trace; trailers are parsed plugin-side (message::parse) so the engine stays §5-agnostic.

Make exec resilient to a transient ETXTBSY (a plugin binary concurrently (re)written by a parallel agent's install) with a bounded retry. Adds the serde_json dependency for the wire.
